description: Where the LLM money is going. Read top-to-bottom: total spend → who's spending → which model → which specialist. Each panel description (hover the ℹ icon) explains exactly what the numbers mean.

panels.title: 💰 Headline — what are we spending overall?

panels.description: Sum of all gen_ai costs across providers (Anthropic Claude + Ollama) over the last 24 hours. This is the bill.
panels.title: 💰 Total LLM spend — last 24h

panels.description: Number of users currently above the 100k tokens/hr cost-anomaly threshold. Red = at least one user is over budget right now. The 'Top spenders' panel below tells you which user(s).
panels.title: ⚠ Heavy users right now (>100k tokens in last hour)

panels.description: **USD per hour** by specialist (5m rate × 3600), stacked. Use to spot which specific agent (sb-router, nc-chatbot, etc.) caused a cost spike. Total stack height = current burn rate × 1h.
panels.title: 💰 Spend rate per specialist — USD per hour

panels.description: Naive extrapolation: current cost-per-second × 24h. NOT a forecast — just 'if nothing changes, this is what tomorrow looks like.' Useful as a sanity check against the actual 24h total above.
panels.title: 🔮 Tomorrow's bill at today's burn rate
👤 Per-user — who is doing the spending?

panels.description: Ten users consuming the most tokens in the last hour, sorted descending. Bars at the right edge = users at or near the 100k anomaly threshold. This is the leaderboard of 'who is spending your AI budget'.
panels.title: 🔥 Top spenders right now (tokens per hour)

panels.description: One row per user who used more than 100,000 tokens in the last hour. Pink bar length = how far over the threshold. Internal employees end in @acme.com; external customers use public mail (gmail.com, etc.).
panels.title: 🚨 Users over the 100k tokens/hour threshold
🤖 Per-model — which model is the bill coming from?

panels.description: **USD per hour** by provider (5m rate × 3600). Most traffic goes to Ollama (~$0/token); a small fraction to Claude. The visible gap is the entire demo's point: routing the right traffic to local models saves money. Red band is Anthropic spend; green is the rounding-error Ollama line.
panels.title: 💸 Claude (paid) vs Ollama (free) — USD per hour

panels.description: Counterfactual: if every Ollama token had instead gone to Claude Haiku ($0.25/M input, $1.25/M output), what would it have cost? This number = (counterfactual Haiku bill) − (actual Ollama bill ≈ $0).
panels.title: 💸 Money saved by routing Ollama instead of Claude Haiku

panels.description: Per-model cost over 24h. Claude Opus is usually #1 even though it serves <1% of traffic. Compare to the call counts in 'AI o11y — Best Models' — expensive ≠ most-used.
panels.title: Which model rang up the bill? (24h)

panels.description: Same breakdown as the 24h table, but as a time series projected to **USD per hour** (5m rate × 3600). Shows when each model fired and how its hourly burn rate moved.
panels.title: 💰 Spend rate per model — USD per hour

panels.description: Donut of 24h cost split by model. Window is 24h so claude-opus-4-7 (fires on ~1% of conversations) is captured even when no opus calls happened in the last hour.
panels.title: Cost share — which model took what share of the bill? (24h)
📦 Per-specialist — which agent's traffic is most expensive?

panels.description: **USD per hour** by <use case, specialist> (5m rate × 3600). Finer-grained than the headline panel — use to compare how nc-chatbot vs sb-router vs nc-gift-finder stack up within their respective apps and use cases. Colors group by specialist: each specialist gets the same hue across every use case.
panels.title: 💰 Spend rate per <use case, specialist> — USD per hour

title: AI o11y — Cost (per-user attribution)
